description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a rectangular wooden exterior and a prominent convex glass screen. The front panel features a large display bordered by a faux woodgrain frame, with two large rotary dials and a speaker grille on the right side. The dials are likely for channel selection and volume control, and there are small colored labels and a brand logo visible beneath them. The sides and top are finished in a dark wood veneer, while the back is black and boxy, housing ventilation slots and various connection ports. The overall design is bulky and solid, typical of mid-to-late 20th-century CRT televisions, and it would be found in homes, garages, or possibly discarded on urban streets. The object exudes a retro aesthetic, with tactile knobs and a robust, utilitarian build.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a woodgrain exterior, convex glass screen, and rotary dials on the front panel. The design is retro, solid, and features a prominent speaker grille and brand logo.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, woodgrain bezel, two rotary dials, speaker grille, and brand logo.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the dark wood veneer panel and the depth of the television's body.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the black, boxy back panel with ventilation slots and connection ports.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the continuous wood veneer surface and the protruding back section.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, faux woodgrain bezel with a rectangular shape and metallic trim, visually distinct and retro-styled.
attribute_control_button_layout: Clustered on the front right panel, including two large rotary dials and a few smaller buttons.
